seoTitle: How to Create an Outlook Distribution List (2026)
metaDescription: Build a Contact Group in Outlook to email your whole team at once, pulling members from your address book or importing an external list.
intro: Typing out every team member's email address each time you send an update wastes time and invites mistakes. Outlook's Contact Groups, formerly known as distribution lists, let you bundle addresses into one reusable group so you can message everyone with a single name in the To field.

steps.heading: Open the People module and start a new group
steps.body: Navigate to your Outlook People workspace and choose the option to create a new Contact Group.

steps.heading: Name your group clearly
steps.body: Give the group a descriptive title that makes it obvious who it reaches, so you don't accidentally send messages to the wrong team later.

steps.heading: Add members from your address book
steps.body: Use the Add Members option to pull contacts from your personal address book or your organization's shared corporate directory.

steps.heading: Import members from an external list
steps.body: If you have addresses from outside Outlook, paste or add them individually using the new email option to include people not already in your contacts.

steps.heading: Save and test the group
steps.body: Save your Contact Group, then open a new email, type the group name in the To field, and confirm all the correct recipients populate.

faq.question: What's the difference between a distribution list and a Contact Group?
faq.answer: They're the same feature; Microsoft renamed distribution lists to Contact Groups in newer versions of Outlook.

faq.question: Can I add people from outside my company to a Contact Group?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add any email address manually, not just contacts already saved in your address book.

faq.question: How do I test that a group works before sending a real email?
faq.answer: Open a new message, type the group's name into the To field, and check that it expands to show every member correctly.

faq.question: Can I edit a Contact Group after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reopen the group at any time to add or remove members as your team changes.
